questions.question: If you plan to host a web application in the Azure platform as a service solution of Azure Web Apps, then the platform will have the ability to scale automatically?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure App Service (formerly Web Apps) is a Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) offering that includes built-in auto-scaling capabilities. You can configure automatic scaling based on metrics like CPU usage, memory usage, or custom rules.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Azure Web Apps do support automatic scaling as a core feature of the App Service platform.

questions.question: You decide to create 2 Virtual machines. Each virtual machine is of the size D2s v3. Would these machines always generate the same monthly cost?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, virtual machines with the same size don't always generate the same monthly cost. Factors like usage time, storage consumed, network traffic, and region affect pricing. If one VM runs more hours than another, costs differ.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because while the compute costs may be similar, additional factors like storage, network egress, and operating hours can cause costs to vary.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that provides a digital online assistant that can provide speech support. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.concept: Azure AI services for conversational interfaces
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure AI Bot Service (Azure Bot Framework) provides digital assistants that can understand and respond to user queries with speech support. It's designed for building conversational AI applications.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Machine Learning is for building ML models, not specifically for bots. Azure IoT Hub connects IoT devices. Azure Functions is for serverless compute, not bot services.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can use past trainings to provide predictions of very high probability. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Machine Learning is a cloud-based service for building, training, and deploying machine learning models. It can use historical data (past trainings) to create predictive models with high probability predictions.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure IoT Hub is for IoT device management. Azure AI Bot is for conversational interfaces. Azure Functions is for serverless code execution.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can provide serverless computing capabilities. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Functions is Azure's serverless computing service that lets you run event-triggered code without having to provision or manage infrastructure. It's designed for serverless workloads.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Machine Learning requires provisioned compute. Azure IoT Hub requires infrastructure. Azure AI Bot can use serverless but isn't primarily a serverless service.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can be used to process data from millions of sensors. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.concept: Azure IoT Hub for sensor data processing
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure IoT Hub is a managed service for bi-directional communication between IoT applications and millions of devices. It can ingest data from millions of sensors and IoT devices.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Machine Learning is for ML model building. Azure AI Bot is for conversational interfaces. Azure Functions is for general serverless code, not specifically designed for IoT sensor data.

questions.question: A company needs to deploy a set of resources to Azure. Below are the key requirements for the deployment: The need to be deployed across several departments. The resources that need to be deployed are all of the same type. You need to recommend a solution to automate the deployment of the Azure resources. Which of the following would you use for this requirement?

explanation.concept: Azure Resource Manager Templates for automation
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Resource Manager Templates are JSON files that define the resources you need to deploy. They enable automated, repeatable deployments across multiple departments with the same resource types.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Virtual Machine scale sets are for scaling VMs. Management Groups are for organizing subscriptions. Microsoft Entra ID is for identity management.

questions.question: Your company needs to deploy an application to virtual machines hosted in Azure. The solution must ensure an SLA of 99.99%. What is the minimum number of virtual machines and availability zones that you need to recommend for the deployment?
options: One virtual machine and One availability zone
Two virtual machines and One availability zone
One virtual machine and Two availability zones
Two virtual machines and Two availability zones

explanation.concept: Azure SLA for VMs and availability zones
explanation.whyCorrect: For 99.99% uptime SLA with VMs, you need at least 2 VMs deployed across 2 or more availability zones. This ensures high availability - if one zone fails, the other zone's VMs continue serving.
explanation.whyIncorrect: One VM cannot achieve 99.99% SLA. One availability zone doesn't provide redundancy. The combination of 2 VMs in 2 zones is the minimum for 99.99%.

questions.question: Your team needs a tool that can be used to correlate events from multiple resources into a central repository. Which of the following can be used for this purpose?

explanation.concept: Azure Log Analytics for centralized logging
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Log Analytics is part of Azure Monitor and can collect, correlate, and analyze log data from multiple Azure resources into a central repository for monitoring and troubleshooting.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Event Hubs is for event streaming. Microsoft Defender for Cloud is for security. Microsoft Entra ID is for identity.

questions.question: A company is planning on hosting an application on an Azure Virtual Machine. It needs to be ensured that the application hosted on the virtual machine is accessible from the Internet over HTTPS. You decide to implement a DDoS protection plan. Would this satisfy the requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, DDoS protection protects against distributed denial-of-service attacks but doesn't configure network access rules. To make a VM accessible over HTTPS, you need to configure Network Security Groups or firewall rules.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because DDoS protection doesn't enable internet access to the VM - it's for protection against attacks, not for configuring accessibility.

questions.question: A company is planning on hosting an application on an Azure Virtual Machine. It needs to be ensured that the application hosted on the virtual machine is accessible from the Internet over HTTPS. You decide to implement an Azure Traffic Manager profile. Would this satisfy the requirement?

explanation.concept: Azure Traffic Manager for DNS-based routing
explanation.whyCorrect: No, Traffic Manager is a DNS-based traffic routing service that doesn't configure network access or enable HTTPS access to VMs. It's for routing traffic, not for enabling access.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Traffic Manager only routes DNS queries and doesn't open ports or configure HTTPS on VMs.

questions.question: Which of the following customers are eligible to use Azure Government to develop a cloud solution? Choose 2 answers from the options given below.

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Government is available to US government entities and their contractors. It's a separate sovereign cloud operated by US government-approved personnel.
explanation.whyIncorrect: European entities are not eligible for Azure Government - they would use regular Azure or Azure China.

questions.question: A team is currently planning on using Azure for hosting resources. They are going to create users which would have access to the Azure resources. They want to implement Multi-Factor authentication for the users. Is it required to deploy a federated solution to implement Multi-Factor authentication?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Azure MFA can be enabled without federation. You can use cloud-based MFA with Microsoft Entra ID, or configure MFA without setting up ADFS federation.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Azure MFA supports cloud-only authentication without requiring federation.

questions.question: A team is currently planning on using Azure for hosting resources. They are going to create users which would have access to the Azure resources. They want to implement Multi-Factor authentication for the users. Are two valid methods for Azure Multi-Factor authentication picture identification and entering a passport number.

explanation.whyCorrect: No, valid MFA methods include: phone call, text message, mobile app notification, or mobile app verification code. Picture identification and passport numbers are not valid MFA methods in Azure.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because picture ID and passport are not among Azure MFA verification methods.

questions.question: Which of the following support plan gives you access to best practice information, health status and notifications, and 24/7 access to billing information at the lowest possible cost.

explanation.whyCorrect: The Standard support plan provides 24/7 access to billing information, health status notifications, and best practice guidance at the lowest cost among plans that include these features.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Basic doesn't include 24/7 billing. Premier is the most expensive. Developer doesn't include 24/7 billing access.

questions.question: If you plan to host a web application in the Azure platform as a service solution of Azure Web Apps, then you will have complete control over the underlying operating system.

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Azure Web Apps is a PaaS offering where Azure manages the OS, runtime, and infrastructure. You don't have direct control over the underlying OS.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because in PaS, Microsoft manages the OS. For full OS control, you would use VMs (IaaS).

questions.question: You decide to create a virtual machine which is of the size D2s_v3. If you plan to stop the virtual machine, will you incur any costs for the storage associated with the virtual machine.

explanation.concept: Azure VM storage costs when stopped
explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, you continue to pay for the storage (managed disks) even when the VM is stopped/deallocated. Only compute costs stop; storage costs continue.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because managed disks are still charged even when the VM is deallocated.

questions.question: Your company is planning on using Azure for hosting Infrastructure level resources such as Azure Virtual Machines. When planning for the costing aspect for these resources, is there a flexibility offered when it comes to Capital and Operational Expenditure.

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure provides flexibility between CapEx and OpEx. You can choose pay-as-you-go (OpEx) or reserved instances (CapEx-like) based on your needs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Azure supports both pricing models - pay-as-you-go and reserved capacity.

questions.question: You are looking at using a Software as a Service in Azure. Which of the following would you be responsible for?

explanation.whyCorrect: In SaaS, you are responsible for configuring the application to meet your needs. The provider handles HA, installation, and scalability.
explanation.whyIncorrect: High availability, installation, and scalability are provider responsibilities in SaaS.

questions.question: A company is planning on migrating all of their on-premises servers to Azure. Their business continuity department has advised that they need to ensure that servers hosted in Azure are available even if an Azure Data Center were to go down. Which of the following is a concept that would be involved in the design stage for this requirement.

explanation.whyCorrect: Fault tolerance ensures that if one data center fails, the application remains available by having redundancy across multiple data centers. This matches the requirement.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Scalability is about handling load. Low latency is about speed. Elasticity is about scaling based on demand. None address data center failure.

questions.question: Under which of the following circumstances could a company look at decommissioning its data center?
options: If they have their infrastructure hosted in a private cloud
If they have their infrastructure hosted in the public cloud
If they have their infrastructure hosted in a hybrid cloud

explanation.whyCorrect: When infrastructure is hosted in the public cloud, companies can decommission their on-premises data centers as cloud services handle the infrastructure.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Private cloud and hybrid cloud still require on-premises infrastructure, so data centers wouldn't be fully decommissioned.

questions.question: A company is planning on migrating a web site to Azure. This is a public web site that is used by users on the Internet. Which of the following must the company plan for if they are planning to migrate the web site to Azure?
options: They would need to deploy a VPN
They would need to pay for the cost to transfer the web site to Azure
They would need to pay for the monthly costs incurred for hosting the web site
They would need to pay for the number of connections to the web site

explanation.whyCorrect: When hosting a public website in Azure, you must plan for monthly hosting costs (compute, storage, bandwidth). Azure is a pay-as-you-go service.
explanation.whyIncorrect: VPN is not required for public websites. There's no transfer cost. Connection-based billing isn't the primary concern.

questions.question: An administrator needs to run a script written in PowerShell. This script is going to create a virtual machine in Azure. Would the script run on a computer that runs Linux and has the Azure CLI tools installed?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, PowerShell scripts cannot run on Linux even with Azure CLI installed. PowerShell scripts require PowerShell, which isn't available on Linux without PowerShell Core.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because PowerShell and Azure CLI are different tools - having CLI doesn't enable PowerShell execution.

questions.question: An administrator needs to run a script written in PowerShell. This script is going to create a virtual machine in Azure. Would you be able to run the script on a machine that has Chrome OS installed and uses Azure Cloud Shell?

explanation.concept: Azure Cloud Shell for cross-platform access
explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure Cloud Shell provides a browser-based shell that supports both PowerShell and Bash. You can run PowerShell scripts from any device with a browser.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Cloud Shell provides PowerShell in the browser, accessible from Chrome OS.

questions.question: An administrator needs to run a script written in PowerShell. This script is going to create a virtual machine in Azure. Would you be able to run the script on a machine that has macOS and PowerShell core installed?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, PowerShell Core (cross-platform PowerShell) can be installed on macOS and can execute PowerShell scripts for Azure management.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because PowerShell Core enables PowerShell execution on macOS.

questions.question: An Administrator needs to view the list of planned maintenance events that could potentially affect the availability of the resources hosted in an Azure subscription. Which of the following blade in the Azure portal should the administrator consider for this requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Advisor provides recommendations and can show planned maintenance events that might affect your resources. It integrates with Service Health.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Resource Groups organize resources. Microsoft Entra ID is for identity. Help + Support is for support requests.

questions.question: Your team needs to have an integrated solution in place that can be used for the deployment of code. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure DevOps provides a complete CI/CD pipeline for building, testing, and deploying code. It's designed for continuous integration and delivery.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Advisor is for recommendations. Azure Cognitive Services is for AI. Azure Application Insights is for monitoring.

questions.question: Your team needs a tool that can provide guidance and recommendations that can be used to improve an Azure environment. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Advisor is a personalized cloud consultant that provides recommendations for improving cost optimization, security, reliability, and performance.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Cognitive Services is for AI APIs. Application Insights is for monitoring. DevOps is for development/deployment.

questions.question: Your team needs to use a tool that can be used to build intelligent Artificial Intelligence based applications. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Cognitive Services provides AI APIs for building intelligent applications, including vision, speech, language, and decision capabilities.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Advisor is for recommendations. Application Insights is for monitoring. DevOps is for deployment.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can be used to monitor web applications deployed to Azure. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Application Insights is an Application Performance Monitoring (APM) service that monitors web applications, detects performance issues, and provides analytics.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Advisor is for recommendations. Cognitive Services is for AI. DevOps is for deployment.

questions.question: A team currently has several computers in their on-premises environment that runs on Windows 10. They need to share files by enabling mapped drives from the computers. Which of the following would be an ideal storage solution for this requirement?
options: Using the Azure storage - BLOB service
Using the Azure storage - File service
Using the Azure storage - Queue service
Using the Azure storage - Table service

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Files provides SMB file shares that can be mapped as drives on Windows computers, making it ideal for the requirement of shared file access with mapped drives.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Blob storage is for object storage. Queue is for messaging. Table is for NoSQL storage.

questions.question: A company wants to have a data store hosted in Azure. Below are the key requirements for the data store: The data store must be able to store JSON documents. The data store must be able to handle data writes from multiple regions. Which of the following would be the ideal data store for this requirement?

explanation.concept: Azure Cosmos DB for global distribution
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Cosmos DB is a globally distributed, multi-model database service that natively supports JSON documents and multi-region writes with automatic replication.
explanation.whyIncorrect: SQL Database has limited multi-region support. Redis Cache is for caching. Synapse is for analytics.

questions.question: A team is planning on deploying Azure Virtual Machines to a resource group named demogroup. The group has been created in the US Central region. Do the virtual machines deployed to the resource group also need to be deployed to the US Central region only?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, resources in a resource group can be deployed to different regions. The resource group only logically organizes resources.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because resource groups don't constrain resource locations.

questions.question: A team is planning on assigning tags to a resource group. Would the tag be inherited by resources in the resource group?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, tags assigned to a resource group are not automatically inherited by resources in that group. Tags must be applied individually or via policy.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because there's no automatic tag inheritance in Azure.

questions.question: A set of IAM permissions have been assigned to a resource group. Would the resources in the resource group automatically inherit the IAM permissions assigned to the resource group?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure RBAC permissions assigned at a resource group level are inherited by all resources within that group.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because RBAC permissions do inherit down to child resources.

questions.question: Which of the following service can be used to build, test and deploy a predictive analytics solution that could subsequently be used to deploy an Artificial Intelligence solution to Azure?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Machine Learning Studio provides a visual interface for building, testing, and deploying predictive analytics and machine learning models.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Functions is for serverless code. Logic Apps is for workflows. Batch is for batch processing.

questions.question: Your team is planning on using the Azure Advisor tool. Would the Azure Advisor tool give recommendations on how to configure network settings for Azure virtual machines?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Azure Advisor doesn't provide network configuration recommendations for VMs. It focuses on cost, security, reliability, and performance.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because network configuration isn't part of Advisor's recommendations.

questions.question: Your team is planning on using the Azure Advisor tool. Would the Azure Advisor tool give recommendations on how to save costs when it comes to hosting virtual machines in Azure?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure Advisor provides cost optimization recommendations, including suggestions for right-sizing VMs and identifying unused resources.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because cost optimization is one of Advisor's primary recommendation categories.

questions.question: Your team is planning on using the Azure Advisor tool. Would the Azure Advisor tool give recommendations on how to improve the security for Microsoft Entra ID?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure Advisor includes security recommendations that can help improve Microsoft Entra ID security posture.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because security recommendations are part of Advisor's offerings.

questions.question: A support engineer currently has a computer that runs Ubuntu. Which of the following Azure Management tools could be run from this computer?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure CLI and Azure Portal can run on Ubuntu. Azure PowerShell requires PowerShell, which needs installation on Linux.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure PowerShell requires PowerShell Core on Linux, which isn't installed by default.

questions.question: Your company has just setup an Azure account and Microsoft Entra ID. They need to ensure that when users connect to Microsoft Entra ID from the Internet from an Anonymous IP address, they are prompted to change their password automatically. Which of the following Azure service can help them achieve this requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: Microsoft Entra ID Protection can detect risky sign-in attempts and automatically prompt users to change passwords when signing in from anonymous IPs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Entra Connect is for directory sync. PIM is for privileged access management. Defender for Identity is for on-premises AD.

questions.question: A company is planning on creating several virtual machines that will be used to host web and database servers. You need to limit the type of connections from the web and database servers. Which of the following can be used to fulfil this requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: NSGs filter network traffic to/from Azure resources and can limit types of connections based on ports, protocols, and IP addresses.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Entra ID Protection is for identity security. VPN is for site-to-site connectivity. Route tables control routing.

questions.question: Which of the following can be used to allow an organization to manage the compliance of resources across multiple subscriptions?

explanation.whyCorrect: Management Groups allow you to organize subscriptions and apply governance policies across multiple subscriptions at scale.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Resource groups organize resources within a subscription. Policies enforce compliance. ARM templates deploy resources.

questions.question: Your company is planning on setting an Azure environment by setting up a subscription and Microsoft Entra ID. They need to purchase a low-cost option support plan. The support plan needs to ensure 24*7 access to support engineers by phone or email. You suggest opting for the Basic Support plan. Does this support the requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, the Basic support plan does not include 24/7 phone or email support. Only billing support is provided.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Basic doesn't provide 24/7 technical support.

questions.question: Your company is planning on setting an Azure environment by setting up a subscription and Microsoft Entra ID. They need to purchase a low-cost option support plan. The support plan needs to ensure 24*7 access to support engineers by phone or email. You suggest opting for the Standard Support plan. Does this support the requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, the Standard support plan provides 24/7 access to support engineers via phone and email.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Standard does provide 24/7 support.

questions.question: Does Microsoft release most of the Azure services in private preview before being introduced in public preview?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, most Azure services are released in private preview first, then public preview, then generally available (GA).
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because this is the standard Azure release lifecycle.

questions.question: Is it true that Azure services released in public preview can only be managed via the Azure command line interface?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, public preview services can be managed via Portal, CLI, PowerShell, and SDKs like any other Azure service.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because preview services are accessible via multiple management tools.

questions.question: Is it true that an Azure service that is released in private preview is made available to all Azure customers?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, private preview services are only available to select customers who are invited to participate.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because private previews are not available to all customers.

questions.question: Is it true that an Azure service that is released in public preview is made available to all Azure customers?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, public preview services are available to all Azure customers to test and provide feedback.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because public previews are available to all customers.

questions.question: Is it true that an Azure service that is released as generally available is made available to all Azure customers?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, generally available services are available to all Azure customers worldwide.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because GA services are available to all customers.

questions.question: Can a single Microsoft Account be used to manage multiple Azure subscriptions?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, a single Microsoft account can manage multiple Azure subscriptions.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because one account can indeed manage multiple subscriptions.

questions.question: Is it possible to merge subscriptions into a single subscription?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Azure subscriptions cannot be merged. You would need to move resources between subscriptions individually.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because subscription merging isn't supported by Azure.

questions.question: A company has just started using Azure. They have setup a subscription and started deploying resources to Azure. Would they be guaranteed a service level agreement of at least 99.9% for paid Azure services?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure provides at least 99.9% SLA for most paid services.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Azure does guarantee 99.9% for paid services.

questions.question: A company has just started using Azure. They have setup a subscription and started deploying resources to Azure. Could the company increase the SLA for their resources by deploying resources across multiple regions?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, deploying across multiple regions can increase availability and potentially achieve higher combined SLAs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because multi-region deployment can improve overall SLA.

questions.question: A company has just started using Azure. They have setup a subscription and started deploying resources to Azure. Could the company increase the SLA for their resources by purchasing multiple subscriptions?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, purchasing more subscriptions doesn't increase the SLA. SLA is determined by service configuration, not subscription count.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because subscriptions don't affect SLA percentages.

questions.question: A set of virtual machines have been created in a subscription. You have now paid for a new subscription. Is it possible to move the virtual machines to the new subscription?

explanation.yes: Yes, you can move Azure VMs to a different subscription using the move functionality.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because VMs can be moved between subscriptions.

questions.question: A company has defined the following resources in Azure: 10 Public IP addresses. 20 users in Microsoft Entra ID. 5 user groups in Microsoft Entra ID. They now want to reduce the costs associated with the Azure account. Would the removal of the users from the account help reduce the cost?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Microsoft Entra ID users don't incur direct costs in Azure. Removing users won't reduce Azure spending.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Entra ID users are free.

questions.question: A company has defined the following resources in Azure: 10 Public IP addresses. 20 users in Microsoft Entra ID. 5 user groups in Microsoft Entra ID. They now want to reduce the costs associated with the Azure account. Would the removal of the user groups from the account help reduce the cost?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Microsoft Entra ID groups don't incur costs. Removing groups won't reduce spending.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Entra ID groups are free.

questions.question: A company has defined the following resources in Azure: 10 Public IP addresses. 20 users in Microsoft Entra ID. 5 user groups in Microsoft Entra ID. They now want to reduce the costs associated with the Azure account. Would the removal of the public IP addresses from the account help reduce the cost?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, unused public IP addresses incur hourly charges. Removing them will reduce costs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because public IPs do have associated costs.

questions.question: A team member currently has a laptop that is based on the Android OS. The team member wants to create a virtual machine in Azure. The team member decides to use the Azure portal to create the virtual machine. Would this suit the purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, the Azure Portal is web-based and can be accessed from any device with a browser, including Android.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because the Portal works on Android devices.

questions.question: A team member currently has a laptop that is based on the Android OS. The team member wants to create a virtual machine in Azure. The team member decides to use the PowerApps portal to create the virtual machine. Would this suit the purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, PowerApps is for building custom business applications, not for creating Azure VMs. The Azure Portal is needed.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because PowerApps isn't used for VM creation.

questions.question: Which of the following URL is used to manage the creation of Azure resources?

explanation.whyCorrect: https://portal.azure.com is the URL for the Azure Portal, used to create and manage Azure resources.
explanation.whyIncorrect: The other URLs don't exist or don't lead to the Azure management portal.

questions.question: You need to create a virtual machine in Azure. Which of the following storage account service is used to store the data disks for the virtual machine?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure VM data disks are stored as page blobs in Azure Storage.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Files is for SMB shares. Tables is for NoSQL. Queues is for messaging.

questions.question: Your company needs to have a data store in Azure. Below are the key requirements for the data store: The data store should be capable of storing 10TB of data. The data would be accessed infrequently. The data would need to be visualised using PowerBI. Which of the following services could you use for this requirement? Choose 2 answers from the options given below.

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Synapse Analytics and Azure Data Lake are designed for large-scale analytics and work well with PowerBI for visualization.
explanation.whyIncorrect: SQL Database has size limitations. Cosmos DB is for operational workloads, not analytics.

questions.question: You need to deploy several virtual machines to Azure. You need to ensure that you can assign permissions to the virtual machines simultaneously. Which of the following can be implemented to fulfil this requirement?
options: Deploy all the virtual machines to the same region
Deploy all the virtual machines to the same resource group
Deploy all the virtual machines to the same availability zone
Deploy all the virtual machines to the same scale

explanation.whyCorrect: Deploying VMs to the same resource group allows you to assign permissions to all VMs at once using resource group-level RBAC.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Region doesn't affect permission grouping. Availability zones are for HA. Scale sets are for scaling.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of moving on-premises servers to the Azure cloud environment?
options: The Azure platform is a public cloud platform wherein all the resources can be accessed by the public
The Azure platform is a private cloud platform wherein all the resources can be accessed only by a private handful of people
The Azure platform is a shared platform wherein multiple companies can use a portion of the resources on the platform
The Azure platform is an e-commerce platform wherein users can buy and sell resources as required

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure is a shared platform where multiple customers share the underlying infrastructure, paying for what they use.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Resources aren't publicly accessible by default. It's not private. It's not an e-commerce platform.

questions.question: What are two key characteristics of the public cloud?

explanation.whyCorrect: Shared infrastructure and pay-as-you-go are key public cloud characteristics.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Dedicated hardware is for private cloud. On-premises is opposite of cloud. Limited availability isn't a characteristic.

questions.question: Your company plans to migrate all its data and resources to Azure. The company migration plan states that only Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions must be used in Azure. You need to deploy an Azure environment that meets the company migration plan. Solution: You create an Azure App Service and Azure SQL databases. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, both Azure App Service and Azure SQL Database are PaaS offerings.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because both are indeed PaaS services.

questions.question: You plan to migrate a web application to Azure. The web application is accessed by external users. You need to recommend a cloud deployment solution to minimize the amount of administrative effort used to manage the web application. What should you include in the recommendation?

explanation.whyCorrect: PaaS minimizes administrative effort because the provider manages the infrastructure, OS, and runtime. You just deploy your code.
explanation.whyIncorrect: SaaS is for complete applications. IaaS requires more management. DaaS is specific to databases.

questions.question: You have an on-premises network that contains 100 servers. You need to recommend a solution that provides additional resources to your users. The solution must minimize capital and operational expenditure costs. What should you include in the recommendation?
options: A complete migration to the public cloud

explanation.whyCorrect: Migrating to public cloud minimizes CapEx (no hardware purchase) and OpEx (pay-as-you-go) costs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Additional data center increases costs. Private cloud still requires capital. Hybrid could work but full migration is simplest.

questions.question: You plan to migrate several servers from an on-premises network to Azure. What is an advantage of using a public cloud service for the servers over an on-premises network?
options: The public cloud is owned by the public, NOT a private corporation
The public cloud is a crowd-sourcing solution that provides corporations with the ability to enhance the cloud
All public cloud resources can be freely accessed by every member of the public
The public cloud is a shared entity whereby multiple corporations each use a portion of the resources in the cloud

explanation.whyCorrect: The public cloud allows multiple organizations to share resources, providing economies of scale and cost efficiency.
explanation.whyIncorrect: It's not owned by the public. It's not crowd-sourcing. Resources aren't publicly accessible by default.

questions.question: You have 1,000 virtual machines hosted on the Hyper-V hosts in a data center. You plan to migrate all the virtual machines to an Azure pay-as-you-go subscription. You need to identify which expenditure model to use for the planned Azure solution. Which expenditure model should you identify?

explanation.whyCorrect: Pay-as-you-go Azure subscriptions operate on an Operational Expenditure model where you pay for what you use.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Elastic refers to scaling. Capital is for upfront purchases. Scalable is a characteristic, not a model.

questions.question: You plan to provision Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) resources in Azure. Which resource is an example of IaaS?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Virtual Machines are IaaS - you manage the VM but Azure manages the underlying infrastructure.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Web Apps, Logic Apps, and SQL Database are all PaaS services.

questions.question: A team of developers at your company plans to deploy, and then remove, 50 virtual machines each week. All the virtual machines are configured by using Azure Resource Manager templates. You need to recommend which Azure service will minimize the administrative effort required to deploy and remove the virtual machines. What should you recommend?

explanation.concept: Azure DevTest Labs for VM management
explanation.whyCorrect: DevTest Labs is designed for dev/test scenarios where VMs are created and removed frequently, minimizing administrative effort.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Reserved Instances are for long-term use. Scale sets are for auto-scaling. Managed Desktop is for end-user devices.

questions.question: Your company plans to migrate all its data and resources to Azure. The company migration plan states that only Platform as a Service (PaaS) solutions must be used in Azure. You need to deploy an Azure environment that meets the company migration plan. Solution: You create an Azure virtual machines, Azure SQL databases, and Azure Storage accounts. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Virtual Machines are IaaS, not PaS. The solution doesn't meet the PaaS-only requirement.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because VMs are not PaaS.

questions.question: Your company plans to deploy several custom applications to Azure. The applications will provide invoicing services to the customers of the company. Each application will have several prerequisite applications and services installed. You need to recommend a cloud deployment solution for all the applications. What should you recommend?

explanation.concept: IaaS for custom applications with dependencies
explanation.whyCorrect: IaaS provides full control including OS and middleware, suitable for applications with multiple dependencies.
explanation.whyIncorrect: SaaS is for ready-made applications. PaaS has more restrictions on dependencies.

questions.question: An Azure region contains one or more data centers that are connected by using a low-latency network.
options: Is found in each country where Microsoft has a subsidiary office
Can be found in every country in Europe and the Americas only
Contains one or more data centers that are connected by using a high-latency network

explanation.whyCorrect: An Azure region is indeed one or more data centers connected with low-latency networking.
explanation.whyIncorrect: The other options are incorrect descriptions of Azure regions.

questions.question: A support engineer plans to perform several Azure management tasks by using the Azure CLI. You install the CLI on a computer. You need to tell the support engineer which tools to use to run the CLI. Which two tools should you instruct the support engineer to use?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure CLI can be run from Command Prompt or PowerShell on Windows.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Resource Explorer is for browsing resources. Firewall and Sharing Center are system utilities.

questions.question: You plan to store 20 TB of data in Azure. The data will be accessed infrequently and visualized by using Microsoft Power BI. You need to recommend a storage solution for the data Which two solutions should you recommend?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Data Lake and Azure Synapse Analytics are designed for large-scale data analytics and work with Power BI.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Cosmos DB is for operational workloads. SQL Database and PostgreSQL are transactional databases.

questions.question: You need to identify the type of failure for which an Azure Availability Zone can be used to protect access to Azure services. What should you identify?

explanation.whyCorrect: Availability Zones protect against Azure data center failures by distributing resources across physically separate data centers.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Server failure is handled by scale sets. Region failure requires geo-replication. Storage failure uses RA-GRS.

questions.question: You have a virtual machine named VM1 that runs Windows Server 2016. VM1 is in the East US Azure region. Which Azure service should you use from the Azure portal to view service failure notifications that can affect the availability of VM1?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Monitor can track service health and send notifications about issues affecting your resources.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Service Fabric is for microservices. VMs is the resource blade. Advisor gives recommendations, not health alerts.

questions.question: An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources. You need to recommend which computer configuration to use to run the script. Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs Linux and has the Azure CLI tools installed. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, PowerShell scripts require PowerShell, not Azure CLI. Azure CLI is a different tool that uses Bash commands.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because CLI and PowerShell are not interchangeable.

questions.question: An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources. You need to recommend which computer configuration to use to run the script. Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs Chrome OS and uses Azure Cloud Shell Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure Cloud Shell provides PowerShell in the browser and can run on Chrome OS via the browser.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Cloud Shell does support PowerShell.

questions.question: You have an Azure environment that contains 10 virtual networks and 100 virtual machines You need to limit the amount of inbound traffic to all the Azure virtual network. What should you create?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Firewall is a managed network security service that can limit inbound traffic to virtual networks.
explanation.whyIncorrect: ASGs group VMs but don't filter traffic. Gateways connect networks. ExpressRoute is for private connections.

questions.question: You have an Azure environment that contains multiple Azure virtual machines. You plan to implement a solution that enables the client computers on your on-premises network to communicate to the Azure virtual machines. You need to recommend which Azure resources must be created for the planned solution. Which two Azure resources should you include in the recommendation?

explanation.whyCorrect: A virtual network is required to host the VMs, and a virtual network gateway enables on-premises to Azure communication.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Load balancer is for traffic distribution. Application gateway is for HTTP traffic. Gateway subnet is part of the gateway.

questions.question: You have an Azure environment. You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system. Solution: You use Bash in Azure Cloud Shell. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure Cloud Shell can be accessed via a browser on Android tablets and provides Bash for VM creation.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Cloud Shell works on any device with a browser.

questions.question: Your company plans to move several servers to Azure. The company compliance policy states that a server named FinServer must be on a separate network segment. You are evaluating which Azure services can be used to meet the compliance policy requirements. Which Azure solution should you recommend?
options: A resource group for FinServer and another resource group for all the other servers
A virtual network for FinServer and another virtual network for all the other servers
A VPN for FinServer and a virtual network gateway for another server
One resource group for all the servers and a resource lock for FinServer

explanation.whyCorrect: Separate virtual networks provide complete network isolation, meeting the requirement for separate network segments.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Resource groups don't provide network isolation. VPNs are for connectivity, not segmentation. Locks prevent changes, not provide isolation.

questions.question: You plan to map a network drive from several computers that run Windows 10 to Azure Storage. You need to create a storage solution in Azure for the planned mapped drive. What should you create?
options: A Files service in a storage account
A Blobs service in a storage account

explanation.concept: Azure Files for Windows mapped drives
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Files provides SMB file shares that can be mapped as network drives on Windows computers.
explanation.whyIncorrect: SQL Database is for databases. VM data disks aren't network shares. Blob storage isn't mountable as drives.

questions.question: Your company plans to migrate all its network resources to Azure. You need to start the planning process by exploring Azure. What should you create first?

explanation.whyCorrect: A subscription is required first - it's the billing container for all Azure resources.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Resource groups, virtual networks, and management groups all require a subscription first.

questions.question: You have an on-premises application that sends email notifications automatically based on a rule, You plan to migrate the application to Azure. You need to recommend a serverless computing solution for the application. What should you include in the recommendation?
options: A server image in Azure Marketplace

explanation.concept: Azure Logic Apps for workflow automation
explanation.whyCorrect: Logic Apps is a serverless service for workflow automation and can send email notifications based on triggers.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Web apps and API apps require code. Marketplace images are for VMs, not serverless.

questions.question: You plan to deploy a website to Azure. The website will be accessed by users worldwide and will host large video files. You need to recommend which Azure feature must be used to provide the best video playback experience. What should you recommend?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure CDN caches content globally at edge locations, providing fast delivery for video files worldwide.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Application Gateway is for app traffic. ExpressRoute is for private connectivity. Traffic Manager is for DNS routing.

questions.question: Your company plans to deploy several million sensors that will upload data to Azure. You need to identify which Azure resources must be created to support the planned solution. Which two Azure resources should you identify?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure IoT Hub connects sensors to the cloud, and Notification Hubs can send commands back to devices.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Data Lake is for analytics. Queue storage is for messaging. File Storage is for file shares.

questions.question: You have an Azure web app. You need to manage the settings of the web app from an iPhone. What are two Azure management tools that you can use?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure CLI (via app) and Azure Portal work on iPhone. Cloud Shell needs browser. PowerShell and Storage Explorer are desktop apps.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Cloud Shell, PowerShell, and Storage Explorer aren't easily accessible on iPhone.

questions.question: Company plans to deploy an Artificial Intelligence (AI) solution in Azure. What should the company use to build, test, and deploy predictive analytics solutions?

explanation.whyCorrect: Machine Learning Studio provides a visual interface for building, testing, and deploying ML models for predictive analytics.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Logic Apps is for workflows. Batch is for batch processing. Cosmos DB is a database.

questions.question: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You sign in to the Azure portal and create a resource group named RG1. From Azure documentation, you have the following command that creates a virtual machine named VM1. az vm create --resource-group RG1 --name VM1 --image UbuntuLTS --generate-ssh-keys. You need to create VM1 in Subscription1 by using the command. Solution: From the Azure portal, launch Azure Cloud Shell and select PowerShell. Run the command in Cloud Shell. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Cloud Shell can run the Azure CLI command in either Bash or PowerShell mode.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Cloud Shell supports running Azure CLI commands.

questions.question: An Azure administrator plans to run a PowerShell script that creates Azure resources. You need to recommend which computer configuration to use to run the script. Solution: Run the script from a computer that runs Windows 10 and has the Azure PowerShell module installed. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Windows 10 with Azure PowerShell module can run PowerShell scripts for Azure management.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because this is a valid configuration.

questions.question: Which service provides serverless computing in Azure?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Functions is Azure's serverless compute service that runs code in response to events without provisioning infrastructure.
explanation.whyIncorrect: VMs require infrastructure. Storage is for data. Container Instances is container hosting, not truly serverless.

questions.question: You have an Azure subscription named Subscription1. You sign in to the Azure portal and create a resource group named RG1. From Azure documentation, you have the following command that creates a virtual machine named VM1. az vm create --resource-group RG1 --name VM1 --image UbuntuLTS --generate-ssh-keys. You need to create VM1 in Subscription1 by using the command. Solution: From the Azure portal, launch Azure Cloud Shell and select Bash. Run the command in Cloud Shell. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Cloud Shell in Bash mode can execute Azure CLI commands including az vm create.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Bash mode supports the Azure CLI.

questions.question: Your company has several business units. Each business unit requires 20 different Azure resources for daily operation. All the business units require the same type of Azure resources. You need to recommend a solution to automate the creation of the Azure resources. What should you include in the recommendations?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Resource Manager templates enable automated, repeatable deployment of identical resources across business units.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Scale sets are for VM scaling. API Management is for API hosting. Management groups organize subscriptions.

questions.question: Which Azure service should you use to collect events from multiple resources into a centralized repository?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Monitor collects metrics and logs from multiple Azure resources into Log Analytics workspaces.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Event Hubs is for event streaming. Analysis Services is for BI. Stream Analytics is for real-time processing.

questions.question: You have an Azure environment. You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system. Solution: You use the PowerApps portal. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, PowerApps is for building business applications, not for creating Azure VMs. The Azure Portal or Cloud Shell is needed.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because PowerApps doesn't create VMs.

questions.question: You have an Azure environment. You need to create a new Azure virtual machine from a tablet that runs the Android operating system. Solution: You use the Azure portal. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, the Azure Portal is web-based and accessible from Android tablets via browser.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because the Portal works on Android.

questions.question: Which Azure service provides a set of version control tools to manage code?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Repos provides Git repositories for version control of source code.
explanation.whyIncorrect: DevTest Labs is for VM dev/test. Storage is for data. Cosmos DB is a database.

questions.question: Your company plans to automate the deployment of servers to Azure. Your manager is concerned that you may expose administrative credentials during the deployment. You need to recommend an Azure solution that encrypts the administrative credentials during the deployment. What should you include in the recommendation?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Key Vault securely stores secrets, keys, and certificates that can be used during deployment without exposing credentials.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Purview is for data governance. Defender is for security monitoring. MFA is for authentication.

questions.question: You plan to deploy several Azure virtual machines. You need to control the ports that devices on the Internet can use to access the virtual machines. What should you use?

explanation.whyCorrect: Network Security Groups filter network traffic and can control which ports are accessible from the internet.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Entra ID roles and groups manage access control, not network ports. Key vaults store secrets.

questions.question: Azure Germany can be used by legal residents of Germany only.
options: Only enterprises that are registered in Germany
Only enterprises that purchase their azure licenses from a partner based in Germany
This service has been discontinued. Azure Germany was retired in 2021.

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Germany was retired in 2021 and replaced with Germany-based regions in the global Azure environment.
explanation.whyIncorrect: The other options describe historical access rules that are no longer relevant.

questions.question: Your Azure environment contains multiple Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP. Solution. You modify a Network Security Group (NSG). Does this meet the goal?

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, NSGs can allow inbound HTTP traffic from the internet to make VMs accessible.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because NSGs can enable HTTP access.

questions.question: Your Azure environment contains multiple Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP. Solution: You modify an Azure firewall. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.yes: Yes, Azure Firewall can be configured to allow HTTP/HTTPS traffic to VMs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Azure Firewall can enable HTTP access.

questions.question: Your Azure environment contains multiple Azure virtual machines. You need to ensure that a virtual machine named VM1 is accessible from the Internet over HTTP. Solution: You modify an Azure Traffic Manager profile. Does this meet the goal?

explanation.concept: Traffic Manager for routing, not access control
explanation.whyCorrect: No, Traffic Manager is a DNS-based routing service and doesn't configure network access rules.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Traffic Manager doesn't control port access.

questions.question: Which two types of customers are eligible to use Azure Government to develop a cloud solution?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Government is available to US government entities and their contractors.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Canadian and European entities aren't eligible for Azure Government.

questions.question: You need to ensure that when Microsoft Entra ID users connect to Microsoft Entra ID from the Internet by using an anonymous IP address, the users are prompted automatically to change their password. Which Azure service should you use?

explanation.whyCorrect: Entra ID Protection can detect risky sign-ins and automatically prompt for password changes when users connect from anonymous IPs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Connect Health monitors hybrid health. PIM manages privileged access. Defender for Identity protects on-premises AD.

questions.question: Your company plans to deploy several web servers and several database servers to Azure. You need to recommend an Azure solution to limit the types of connections from the web servers to the database servers. What should you include in the recommendation?

explanation.whyCorrect: NSGs can filter traffic between web servers and database servers, limiting connection types.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Service Bus is for messaging. Local network gateway is for VPN. Route filter is for ExpressRoute.

questions.question: To what should an application connect to retrieve security tokens?

explanation.concept: Microsoft Entra ID for token issuance
explanation.whyCorrect: Applications connect to Microsoft Entra ID to obtain security tokens for authentication and authorization.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Storage stores data. Certificate stores hold certificates. Key Vault stores secrets and keys.

questions.question: Which service provides network traffic filtering across multiple Azure subscriptions and virtual networks?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Firewall provides centralized network traffic filtering across multiple subscriptions and VNets.
explanation.whyIncorrect: ASGs are for VM grouping. DDoS protection is for attack mitigation. NSGs are per subnet/VNet.

questions.question: Which Azure service should you use to store certificates?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Key Vault can store and manage certificates, keys, and secrets securely.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Defender for Cloud is for security. Storage is for data. Purview is for data governance.

questions.question: You have a resource group named RG1. You plan to create virtual networks and app services in RG1. You need to prevent the creation of virtual machines only in RG1. What should you use?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Policy can deny creation of specific resource types (like VMs) while allowing other resources.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Locks prevent deletion. Roles grant permissions. Tags are for organization.

questions.question: What can Microsoft Purview Information Protection encrypt?

explanation.whyCorrect: Purview Information Protection encrypts documents and emails to protect sensitive information.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Network traffic uses TLS. Storage accounts use encryption at rest. SQL Database uses TDE.

questions.question: From Azure Monitor, you can view which user turned off a specific virtual machine during the last 14 days.

explanation.concept: Azure Activity Log for user actions
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Activity Log records all management operations including who performed actions like stopping VMs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Event Hubs is for streaming. Service Health shows service issues.

questions.question: Your company has an Azure subscription that contains resources in several regions. A company policy states that administrators must only be allowed to create additional Azure resources in a region in the country where their office is located. You need to create the Azure resource that must be used to meet the policy requirement. What should you create?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Policy can restrict resource creation to specific regions based on office locations.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Locks prevent deletion. Management groups organize subscriptions. Reservations are for capacity.

questions.question: From Azure Cloud Shell, you can track your company regulatory standards and regulations, such as ISO 27001.

explanation.whyCorrect: The Trust Center provides access to compliance information including ISO 27001 and other regulatory standards.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Cloud Shell is for management. Partner Portal is for partners. Compliance Manager is for M365.

questions.question: You need to configure an Azure solution that meets the following requirements: Secures websites from attacks. Generates reports that contain details of attempted attacks. What should you include in the solution?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Firewall can secure web applications and generate logs/reports about detected attacks.
explanation.whyIncorrect: NSG is for basic filtering. Purview is for data protection. DDoS is for volumetric attacks.

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure App Service (formerly Web Apps) is a Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) offering that includes built-in auto-scaling capabilities. You can configure automatic scaling based on metrics like CPU usage, memory usage, or custom rules.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Azure Web Apps do support automatic scaling as a core feature of the App Service platform.

questions.question: If you plan to host a web application in the Azure platform as a service solution of Azure Web Apps, then the platform will have the ability to scale automatically?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, virtual machines with the same size don't always generate the same monthly cost. Factors like usage time, storage consumed, network traffic, and region affect pricing. If one VM runs more hours than another, costs differ.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because while the compute costs may be similar, additional factors like storage, network egress, and operating hours can cause costs to vary.

questions.question: You decide to create 2 Virtual machines. Each virtual machine is of the size D2s v3. Would these machines always generate the same monthly cost?

explanation.concept: Azure AI services for conversational interfaces
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure AI Bot Service (Azure Bot Framework) provides digital assistants that can understand and respond to user queries with speech support. It's designed for building conversational AI applications.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Machine Learning is for building ML models, not specifically for bots. Azure IoT Hub connects IoT devices. Azure Functions is for serverless compute, not bot services.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that provides a digital online assistant that can provide speech support. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Machine Learning is a cloud-based service for building, training, and deploying machine learning models. It can use historical data (past trainings) to create predictive models with high probability predictions.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure IoT Hub is for IoT device management. Azure AI Bot is for conversational interfaces. Azure Functions is for serverless code execution.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can use past trainings to provide predictions of very high probability. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Functions is Azure's serverless computing service that lets you run event-triggered code without having to provision or manage infrastructure. It's designed for serverless workloads.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Machine Learning requires provisioned compute. Azure IoT Hub requires infrastructure. Azure AI Bot can use serverless but isn't primarily a serverless service.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can provide serverless computing capabilities. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.concept: Azure IoT Hub for sensor data processing
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure IoT Hub is a managed service for bi-directional communication between IoT applications and millions of devices. It can ingest data from millions of sensors and IoT devices.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Machine Learning is for ML model building. Azure AI Bot is for conversational interfaces. Azure Functions is for general serverless code, not specifically designed for IoT sensor data.

questions.question: Your team needs to have a tool that can be used to process data from millions of sensors. Which of the following service can be used for this purpose?

explanation.concept: Azure Resource Manager Templates for automation
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Resource Manager Templates are JSON files that define the resources you need to deploy. They enable automated, repeatable deployments across multiple departments with the same resource types.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Virtual Machine scale sets are for scaling VMs. Management Groups are for organizing subscriptions. Microsoft Entra ID is for identity management.

questions.question: A company needs to deploy a set of resources to Azure. Below are the key requirements for the deployment: The need to be deployed across several departments. The resources that need to be deployed are all of the same type. You need to recommend a solution to automate the deployment of the Azure resources. Which of the following would you use for this requirement?

explanation.concept: Azure SLA for VMs and availability zones
explanation.whyCorrect: For 99.99% uptime SLA with VMs, you need at least 2 VMs deployed across 2 or more availability zones. This ensures high availability - if one zone fails, the other zone's VMs continue serving.
explanation.whyIncorrect: One VM cannot achieve 99.99% SLA. One availability zone doesn't provide redundancy. The combination of 2 VMs in 2 zones is the minimum for 99.99%.

options: One virtual machine and One availability zone
Two virtual machines and One availability zone
One virtual machine and Two availability zones
Two virtual machines and Two availability zones
questions.question: Your company needs to deploy an application to virtual machines hosted in Azure. The solution must ensure an SLA of 99.99%. What is the minimum number of virtual machines and availability zones that you need to recommend for the deployment?

explanation.concept: Azure Log Analytics for centralized logging
explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Log Analytics is part of Azure Monitor and can collect, correlate, and analyze log data from multiple Azure resources into a central repository for monitoring and troubleshooting.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Azure Event Hubs is for event streaming. Microsoft Defender for Cloud is for security. Microsoft Entra ID is for identity.

questions.question: Your team needs a tool that can be used to correlate events from multiple resources into a central repository. Which of the following can be used for this purpose?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, DDoS protection protects against distributed denial-of-service attacks but doesn't configure network access rules. To make a VM accessible over HTTPS, you need to configure Network Security Groups or firewall rules.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because DDoS protection doesn't enable internet access to the VM - it's for protection against attacks, not for configuring accessibility.

questions.question: A company is planning on hosting an application on an Azure Virtual Machine. It needs to be ensured that the application hosted on the virtual machine is accessible from the Internet over HTTPS. You decide to implement a DDoS protection plan. Would this satisfy the requirement?

explanation.concept: Azure Traffic Manager for DNS-based routing
explanation.whyCorrect: No, Traffic Manager is a DNS-based traffic routing service that doesn't configure network access or enable HTTPS access to VMs. It's for routing traffic, not for enabling access.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Traffic Manager only routes DNS queries and doesn't open ports or configure HTTPS on VMs.

questions.question: A company is planning on hosting an application on an Azure Virtual Machine. Would this satisfy the requirement?

explanation.whyCorrect: Azure Government is available to US government entities and their contractors. It's a separate sovereign cloud operated by US government-approved personnel.
explanation.whyIncorrect: European entities are not eligible for Azure Government - they would use regular Azure or Azure China.

questions.question: Which of the following customers are eligible to use Azure Government to develop a cloud solution? Choose 2 answers from the options given below.

explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because Azure MFA supports cloud-only authentication without requiring federation.

questions.question: A team is currently planning on using Azure for hosting resources. They are going to create users which would have access to the Azure resources. They want to implement Multi-Factor authentication for the users. Is it required to deploy a federated solution to implement Multi-Factor authentication?

explanation.whyCorrect: No, valid MFA methods include: phone call, text message, mobile app notification, or mobile app verification code. Picture identification and passport numbers are not valid MFA methods in Azure.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because picture ID and passport are not among Azure MFA verification methods.

questions.question: A team is currently planning on using Azure for hosting resources. They are going to create users which would have access to the Azure resources. They want to implement Multi-Factor authentication for the users. Are two valid methods for Azure Multi-Factor authentication picture identification and entering a passport number.

explanation.whyCorrect: The Standard support plan provides 24/7 access to billing information, health status notifications, and best practice guidance at the lowest cost among plans that include these features.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Basic doesn't include 24/7 billing. Premier is the most expensive. Developer doesn't include 24/7 billing access.

questions.question: Which of the following support plan gives you access to best practice information, health status and notifications, and 24/7 access to billing information at the lowest possible cost.

explanation.whyCorrect: No, Azure Web Apps is a PaaS offering where Azure manages the OS, runtime, and infrastructure. You don't have direct control over the underlying OS.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Yes is incorrect because in PaS, Microsoft manages the OS. For full OS control, you would use VMs (IaaS).

questions.question: If you plan to host a web application in the Azure platform as a service solution of Azure Web Apps, then you will have complete control over the underlying operating system.

explanation.concept: Azure VM storage costs when stopped
explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, you continue to pay for the storage (managed disks) even when the VM is stopped/deallocated. Only compute costs stop; storage costs continue.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because managed disks are still charged even when the VM is deallocated.

questions.question: You decide to create a virtual machine which is of the size D2s_v3. If you plan to stop the virtual machine, will you incur any costs for the storage associated with the virtual machine.

explanation.whyCorrect: Yes, Azure provides flexibility between CapEx and OpEx. You can choose pay-as-you-go (OpEx) or reserved instances (CapEx-like) based on your needs.
explanation.whyIncorrect: No is incorrect because Azure supports both pricing models - pay-as-you-go and reserved capacity.

questions.question: Your company is planning on using Azure for hosting Infrastructure level resources such as Azure Virtual Machines. When planning for the costing aspect for these resources, is there a flexibility offered when it comes to Capital and Operational Expenditure.

explanation.whyCorrect: In SaaS, you are responsible for configuring the application to meet your needs. The provider handles HA, installation, and scalability.
explanation.whyIncorrect: High availability, installation, and scalability are provider responsibilities in SaaS.

questions.question: You are looking at using a Software as a Service in Azure. Which of the following would you be responsible for?

explanation.whyCorrect: Fault tolerance ensures that if one data center fails, the application remains available by having redundancy across multiple data centers. This matches the requirement.
explanation.whyIncorrect: Scalability is about handling load. Low latency is about speed. Elasticity is about scaling based on demand. None address data center failure.

questions.question: A company is planning on migrating all of their on-premises servers to Azure. Their business continuity department has advised that they need to ensure that servers hosted in Azure are available even if an Azure Data Center were to go down.
